vitals.terrain: gravel roads, single-track, jungle, volcanoes, rivers
vitals.participation_trend: international draw with veterans and newcomers
vitals.location: San José to Limón, Costa Rica

course_description.overview: Three-day coast-to-coast mountain bike stage race from Pacific (Jacó) to Caribbean (Limón), traversing rainforests, volcanoes, rivers, and extreme elevation changes up to 3,000m.

key_segments.name: Day 1: Jacó to San José
key_segments.description: Brutal climbs through muddy rainforest, river crossings, 20,000+ ft elevation gain, 17-25% inclines in heat and humidity.

key_segments.name: Day 2: San José to Turrialba
key_segments.description: Details coming soon; continues through volcanic terrain and changing microclimates.

key_segments.name: Day 3: Turrialba to Limón (return to Turrialba)
key_segments.description: Descent to Caribbean coast through jungle and wild nature; details coming soon.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Known as the world's toughest mountain bike race with relentless climbs, technical terrain, heat, humidity, and diverse ecosystems pushing riders to physical and mental limits.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into San José (SJO) or Jacó area; race starts Pacific coast, ends near Limón with return.
logistics.accommodation: Organized stage race with packages; hotels in San José, Turrialba likely included in entries.
logistics.registration_details: Tico Package through May 30, 2026 (max 100); payment plans to Sept 2026; various categories including Open age groups and Elite.
logistics.bike_logistics: Mountain bikes required; transport between stages organized.
logistics.local_considerations: Extreme heat/humidity, river crossings, jungle conditions; prepare for biodiversity, volcanoes, and microclimates.

final_verdict.one_liner: Legendary brutal coast-to-coast MTB epic across Costa Rica's wild heart.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Hardcore endurance MTB riders seeking ultimate adventure and suffering.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those chasing prestige and notable challenge.
pro_tips: Train for 20,000+ ft/day climbs and heat acclimation.
Opt for Tico Package early (limited spots).
Pack for mud, rivers, and jungle—fat tires important.
Embrace the push-bike sections; spirit-breaking inclines await.

videos.title: Razones para comprar una bici de Aluminio
videos.description:
¿Qué razones podemos encontrar a la hora de comprarnos una bici de aluminio? ¿Qué diferencias hay con las bicis de carbono?
Hoy analizamos el por qué podemos comprar bicis de aluminio.

00:28 Más económico
01:12 Aluminio de gama superior mejor que el carbono barato 
02:12 Más durabilidad 
03:23 El aluminio es reciclable.
04:09 El peso sobrevalorado 
05:20 Mejores componentes
06:21 Rigidez y comodidad
07:25 Bikepaking
